Category: Subsidiary of public technology company (enterprise collaboration / AI work platform)
Overview of the Company: Slack Technologies, LLC operates the Slack platform — a channel-based messaging, collaboration, and AI work platform used by over 200,000 organizations. Acquired by Salesforce in 2021 for $27.7 billion, Slack has evolved into an "AI-first" interface for work, integrating messaging, huddles, file sharing, workflows, and agentic AI (Slackbot). It serves as a central hub connecting teams with tools like Salesforce CRM, with strong enterprise adoption and recent AI-heavy feature updates for automation and productivity.
Company Headquarters: 500 Howard Street, San Francisco, CA 94105, USA
Countries Operating In: United States; Canada; United Kingdom; Ireland; Australia; Japan; Germany; France; India; Brazil
Office Locations: New York, NY; Denver, CO; London, UK; Dublin, IE; Melbourne, AU; Tokyo, JP; Paris, FR; Berlin, DE; Pune, IN; Vancouver, CA
Vision: To make work life simpler, more pleasant, and more productive.
Mission: To be the AI work platform where work happens — connecting people, tools, and information.
Values: Empathy; Courtesy; Thriving on Change; Craftsmanship; Playfulness; Solidarity
Interesting Facts: Slack began as an internal tool for a gaming company named Tiny Speck; The name stands for "Searchable Log of All Communication and Knowledge."
Recent News: Launch of "Agentic Slack" platform (Sept 2025); Integration with Salesforce Agentforce (Feb 2026); Surpassing 1 million active apps used weekly (March 2026)
Regulatory & Compliance Status: HIPAA Compliant; GDPR Compliant; SOC2 Type II; ISO 27001; FedRAMP Authorized
Legal Issues / Controversies: Historical antitrust complaints vs Microsoft; Scrutiny over "Slack AI" data usage policies (2024-2025)
ESG Practices or Ratings: Salesforce Net Zero Cloud integration; Carbon-neutral operations; Strong focus on ethical AI guardrails
Supply Chain Dependencies: AWS (Core Infrastructure); Salesforce Infrastructure; NVIDIA (AI compute)

Macro Risks: Consolidation in SaaS market; High interest rates affecting IT spend; Competition from free platform bundles

Weaknesses / Gaps in Offering: Video quality lag vs Zoom/Teams; Complexity in large-scale channel management; High cost for premium tiers; Over-reliance on the Salesforce ecosystem
